Bikaner Police sends a strong and unmistakable message to drug traffickers under SP Mridul Kachhawa’s “Operation Neelkanth.” In a major anti-narcotics crackdown, the illegal encroachment constructed outside the residence of alleged drug peddler Mehdi Hasan in Bhutto Ka Bas was demolished using bulldozers.

More than 100 police personnel, including women officers, were deployed during the operation under the supervision of SHO Surendra Pachhar and Bikaner Development Authority officials. Mehdi Hasan reportedly faces five NDPS cases, while one case is also registered against his wife.

The action reflects Rajasthan Chief Minister Bhajanlal Sharma’s zero-tolerance policy against narcotics. The message is loud and clear: those poisoning the youth and destroying society through drugs will face not only legal action but also strict administrative consequences.

tweets.title:
J-K: Indian Army launches community radio 'Sangam 88.8' to empower youth in Rajouri border area
Rajouri (Jammu and Kashmir):  In an excellent initiative, the Indian Army has established a community radio "Sangam 88.8" in the Doongi block of the border district Rajouri. It promotes local culture and offers opportunities to the youth living in the border area.

RJ Sana expressed her joy, saying that the community radio has a great significance because the people living in the border areas typically do not recieve crucial information, government schemes, and other resources. She noted that around 200-250 candidates applied for the job.

But then I saw his post criticising an IAF official for pointing out how India controlled the escalation matrix, showcased strong political will, and used indigenous weapons. I mean that's the most evident thing in the whole op apart from destroyed Pakistani bases.

He claims the most successful weapons in Op Sindoor were the S400 and Brahmos. Both have Russian involvement, but that's a very myopic view of the whole operation.

The S400 did what it was bought for with top dollars. Brahmos did its job normally. Any disciplined and potent air defense unit could have brought down at least a few Brahmos in a real-world scenario. Pakistanis are not in that category.

The most important weapon, which showcased the vision of the Indian Armed Forces along with the Indian military defence complex, was the L70.

That thing was the hero of Op Sindoor, at least as per my assessment with limited data on engagements during the conflict.

The L70 is an Indian brainchild. Many such guns exist across the world, but they were never deployed and utilised in the way the L70 was. The important thing about it is that the L70 was developed into a drone killer with a vision for a kind of threat that such a system has hardly faced or countered.

And if these guys can discount IACCS, Akashteer system and the whole group of indigenous developed radar and optical surveillance systems just to belittle the Atmanirbhar initiative, then we can assume why they are being selective.

I am very much sure that these guys get editorial space or media platforms just because either they have been around for a couple of decades or their parents were in some important position.

R to @NetramDefence: The first test was planned on 11 May. The thermonuclear device was placed in a shaft code named White House, which was approximately 230m deep, the fission bomb was placed in a 150m deep shaft code named Taj Mahal, and the first sub-kiloton device in shaft Kumbhkaran.
The timing of the tests was pursuant to the local weather conditions, and the test sequence was initiated in the afternoon.

The devices were delivered to the device preparation building, which was designated as Prayer Hall.
